chief_complaint: A 27-year-old male with type 1 diabetes mellitus presented with diabetic ketoacidosis, severe trismus, maxillofacial swelling, cervical rigidity, and dyspnea.
history: The patient presented with a 2-day history of worsening diabetic ketoacidosis, severe trismus, and maxillofacial swelling. He had a history of type 1 diabetes mellitus and had been non-compliant with his insulin regimen. He had no significant past medical history.
exam: Vital signs: pH 6.996, glucose 22.2 mmol/L, temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, heart rate 110 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min, oxygen saturation 88% on room air. Physical examination revealed severe trismus (interincisal distance <1.5 cm), maxillofacial swelling, cervical rigidity, and dyspnea.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: pH 6.996, glucose 22.2 mmol/L, sodium 135 mmol/L, potassium 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL. Imaging: Magnetic resonance imaging confirmed multispace infection with cervical abscess, mediastinal extension, and pleural effusion.
correct_diagnosis: Life-threatening multispace infection of the floor of the mouth complicated by diabetic ketoacidosis, cervical abscess, and imminent airway obstruction
differentials: Life-threatening multispace infection of the floor of the mouth complicated by diabetic ketoacidosis, cervical abscess, and imminent airway obstruction
Diabetic ketoacidosis with severe trismus and multispace infection
Cervical abscess with mediastinal extension and pleural effusion
Maxillofacial infection with trismus and airway compromise
correct_ranking: Life-threatening multispace infection of the floor of the mouth complicated by diabetic ketoacidosis, cervical abscess, and imminent airway obstruction
Diabetic ketoacidosis with severe trismus and multispace infection
Cervical abscess with mediastinal extension and pleural effusion
Maxillofacial infection with trismus and airway compromise
